query: ok we have 11 features on the roadmap for q2, leadership wants all of them but that's not happening. how do I figure out which ones actually matter vs which ones are just on there because someone was loud in the planning meeting
I shipped 4 features last quarter and honestly I'm not sure 2 of them were the right call. is there a way to run a pressure test on what we already built to see if it was worth it?

we're a 6-person startup and everyone on the team has different ideas about what to build next. designer wants to redo onboarding. engineer wants to pay off technical debt. sales is screaming about SSO. how do I cut through this without just picking the loudest voice?

before we start q4 planning I want to understand what gaps exist in our product that we're probably blind to. we've been so focused on shipping that we might be missing something structural. can you help me see it?

my PM intuition says we should build the referral feature next but I can't explain why in a way that satisfies our CTO. is there a framework for validating whether a feature has structural right to exist vs is just a good idea that sounds good

we keep shipping features that get low adoption and I don't know why — the user research said people wanted them. something is off between what users say they want and what they actually use. help me figure out what's actually driving demand

I want to know if the features we're planning to ship will survive competitive pressure once they're out there. we have three direct competitors and they move fast. how do I know if what we're building is defensible
can you pressure test our current feature roadmap: we have (1) dark mode, (2) bulk export, (3) team permissions, (4) mobile app, (5) zapier integration. small team, 10 weeks, b2b saas

run a competitive pressure analysis on our Q3 feature list. we're building a hiring tool and the features are: structured interview kits, scorecard builder, calendar sync, slack notifications, and a Chrome extension for sourcing
I want to use the CPA framework on our product backlog before the next sprint planning
our analytics show that 3 of the features we shipped last year have under 10% adoption. I want to understand if they were wrong features or just poorly implemented. help me figure out which
can you help me write a user story for the new notification feature we're building? it needs to cover the happy path and a few edge cases for the engineering team
what's the best way to run a user interview? we're talking to 8 customers next week and I want to make sure we're asking the right questions to understand their workflow
I need to write a PRD for our search feature. the main requirements are full-text search across documents, filter by date range, and the ability to save searches. can you draft this
we're thinking about pivoting from B2B to B2C. what are the main things we'd need to consider? pricing model, distribution, support load, etc
can you summarize the jobs-to-be-done framework for me? I need to explain it to my team in our next all-hands and want to make sure I have it right
write me a competitive analysis comparing our product to Notion, Linear, and Asana. focus on pricing, features, and target customer
help me design an A/B test for our checkout flow. we want to test removing the promo code field to see if it increases conversion
what metrics should I be tracking to know if our product is healthy? I'm thinking retention, NPS, and maybe something about feature adoption but I'm not sure what the right dashboard looks like
I want to do a GMR navigation on our product positioning — specifically the thesis that 'ease of use is not a moat'. can you run that through the toroidal framework?
